title: Getting Started with pakhom
text: pakhom R package automates reflexive thematic analysis (Braun & Clarke, 2006) using AI. takes dataset text entries (e.g., forum posts, survey responses, interview transcripts) produces publication-quality HTML report containing themes, codes, sentiment analysis, correlation analysis, supporting evidence. give data research question. gives back complete thematic analysis – including kinds tables, figures, statistical tests normally produce hand weeks manual coding.

title: Getting Started with pakhom
text: Researchers : conducting qualitative mixed-methods research large text datasets Want use AI assist (replace) analytical process May may deep experience R programming Want reproducible, transparent, auditable thematic analysis

title: Getting Started with pakhom
text: package runs pipeline mirrors manual reflexive thematic analysis works: Codebook-first learning – Learns previous manual analyses (QDPX/NVivo codebooks, Excel, CSV) including full code hierarchy, entry-level coding examples, discarded codes Data loading – Reads SQLite database (one tables) Progressive sequential coding – AI reads entry one time, coding applicable text segments skipping irrelevant content. Builds growing codebook goes (relevance filter, batch coding, consolidation step) Researcher review (optional) – curate codebook theming Code-aware sentiment analysis – AI assigns sentiment scores using codes assigned entry context accurate scoring Iterative bottom-theme generation – Codes merged multiple passes productive groupings exist. Themes subthemes emerge organically merge depth Deterministic theme cascading – Entry--theme assignment flows code hierarchy (pure R, AI re-reading entries) Correlation analysis – Statistical tests themes variables Report generation – Produces interactive HTML report multiple points, pipeline can pause let review modify decisions proceeding. can also stop resume time – system tracks entries processed continues left .

title: Getting Started with pakhom
text: pakhom uses AI services analyze text. need API key one providers: OpenAI (recommended beginners): Sign platform.openai.com. Go API Keys create one. look like sk-proj-.... Anthropic: Sign console.anthropic.com. Create API key. look like sk-ant-.... services charge per use. typical analysis ~500 entries costs approximately $2–5 USD. Important: Never share API key commit version control. package reads environment variable (explained ).

title: Getting Started with pakhom
text: text data must SQLite database (.db file). data currently CSV, Excel, another format, can convert : row table represent one text entry (e.g., one forum post, one survey response). need minimum column containing text content.
code: # Convert a CSV to SQLite library(DBI) library(RSQLite)  db <- dbConnect(SQLite(), "my_data.db") my_data <- read.csv("my_data.csv") dbWriteTable(db, "posts", my_data) dbDisconnect(db)

text: package includes optional Reddit scraper (scrape_reddit()) can populate SQLite database directly. don’t need data already prepared. want use , note November 2025 Reddit longer offers self-service API key creation — must first approved Reddit’s Responsible Builder Program: Review policy: https://support.reddithelp.com/hc/en-us/articles/42728983564564-Responsible-Builder-Policy File support ticket describing use case (subreddits, expected request volume, whether commercial vs academic). Target turnaround ~7 days academic / non-commercial research. sensitive-population subreddits (e.g. anything health-related), Reddit reviewers significantly favor applications cite IRB approval exemption letter institution. approved, store credentials .Renviron: REDDIT_CLIENT_ID=your_id_here     REDDIT_CLIENT_SECRET=your_secret_here     REDDIT_USER_AGENT=pakhom/1.0.0 (u/YourRedditUsername) config: scraping.enabled: true plus list subreddits. approval pending ’d rather skip scraper, just leave scraping.enabled: false (default) load data SQLite .db file using whatever method prefer.

title: Getting Started with pakhom
text: Create file called config.yaml project directory. minimal example:
code: study:   name: "My Research Study"   research_focus: "How does medication affect the interplay between sleep and binge eating?"   research_context: "Online forum discussions from Reddit"   concepts:     - "medication"     - "sleep"     - "binge eating"  ai:   provider: "openai"   openai:     api_key_env: "OPENAI_API_KEY"  data:   database: "my_data.db"   tables: "posts"   source_type: "reddit"  output:   results_dir: "outputs/results"   generate_report: true

title: Getting Started with pakhom
text: study.research_focus – important setting. tells AI research question . specific. Instead “sleep problems,” write “medication affect interplay sleep quality binge eating behaviors?” study.concepts – key concepts study. help AI generate targeted codes focused research question. List 2–5 concepts. ai.provider – Either "openai" "anthropic". OpenAI’s GPT-4o default works well studies. data.source_type – Tells package preprocess data. Options: "reddit", "twitter", "clinical", "generic". unsure, use "generic".

title: pakhom: AI-Integrated Reflexive Thematic Analysis
text: AI-Integrated Reflexive Thematic Analysis Following Braun & Clarke (2006) pakhom R package automates reflexive thematic analysis using OpenAI Anthropic APIs. takes dataset text entries (forum posts, survey responses, interview transcripts) produces publication-quality HTML report containing themes, codes, sentiment analysis, correlation analysis, supporting evidence. give data research question. gives back complete thematic analysis – including kinds tables, figures, statistical tests normally produce hand weeks manual coding.

title: pakhom: AI-Integrated Reflexive Thematic Analysis
text: Progressive sequential coding – entries read one time, just human researcher NVivo. AI codes applicable text segments inline, building reusing codebook organically goes Thematic saturation detection – triangulated monitoring (code creation rate, reuse stability, AI self-assessment) stops coding new patterns emerging, saving time cost Iterative bottom-theme generation – codes merged clusters sequential passes productive groupings exist. Themes subthemes emerge organically data, top-labels Deterministic code-path cascading – entries map themes codes (AI re-reading raw text), faithful inductive process Code-aware sentiment analysis – sentiment scored coding, using assigned codes context accurate emotional valence detection Codebook-first learning prior studies – learns coding conventions, structural relationships, discarded patterns QDPX codebooks (NVivo), Excel, CSV files. Manuscripts serve supplementary clarification Researcher review points – pause pipeline coding theme generation curate AI’s output continuing Checkpoint/resume – long-running analyses can interrupted resumed without losing progress. Flat checkpoint architecture ensures reliable resume across retries Inter-rater reliability – built-IRR computation (Cohen’s kappa, Krippendorff’s alpha) human verification AI-generated codes Multiple AI providers – works OpenAI (GPT-4o) Anthropic (Claude), optional parallel multi-model mode cross-provider analysis Inter-model reliability – run pipeline different AI models compare results via compare_models() compute inter-model agreement (Cohen’s kappa, theme similarity, sentiment correlation) Embedding-augmented theme emergence – uses text embeddings code co-occurrence statistics provide quantitative context iterative code merging, complementing AI’s semantic judgment Researcher reflexivity – injects researcher positionality, research paradigm, reflexive notes AI prompts, aligning Braun & Clarke’s emphasis reflexive practice AI decision audit trail – every AI decision (code assignments, merges, skips, saturation signals) logged JSONL file full rationale post-hoc transparency review QDPX export – exports codebook coded segments QDPX format import NVivo, ATLAS.ti, MAXQDA manual verification Longitudinal analysis – entries timestamps, tracks theme prevalence trends emergence timelines within single run Publication-quality reports – generates self-contained HTML reports theme narratives, representative quotes, sentiment breakdowns, saturation curves, correlation tables, confidence intervals, decision transparency Run comparison – compare results across pipeline runs assess stability track themes evolve

title: pakhom: AI-Integrated Reflexive Thematic Analysis
code: # Install from GitHub devtools::install_github("abanoub-armanious/pakhom")  # Set your API key in .Renviron usethis::edit_r_environ() # Add: OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-your-key-here  # Option A: Interactive config wizard (web UI) pakhom::config_wizard_app()  # Option B: CLI config wizard pakhom::config_wizard()  # Option C: Create config programmatically pakhom::create_config(   study_name = "My Study",   research_focus = "How does X relate to Y?",   database_path = "my_data.db",   output_path = "config.yaml" )  # Run the analysis results <- pakhom::run_analysis("config.yaml")

what: pakhom: AI-Assisted Reflexive Thematic Analysis — pakhom-package
title: pakhom: AI-Assisted Reflexive Thematic Analysis — pakhom-package
text: Conducts AI-assisted reflexive thematic analysis following Braun & Clarke's approach. Uses progressive sequential coding, iterative bottom-theme generation, deterministic code-path cascading. Supports OpenAI Anthropic providers, codebook-first learning prior studies, checkpoint/resume, publication-quality HTML reporting.

title: pakhom: AI-Assisted Reflexive Thematic Analysis — pakhom-package
text: run_analysis — orchestrates full pipeline YAML config.

title: pakhom: AI-Assisted Reflexive Thematic Analysis — pakhom-package
text: Progressive sequential coding (entries read one time, like NVivo) Thematic saturation detection (triangulated: code creation rate, reuse stability, AI self-assessment) Iterative bottom-theme generation (codes merged clusters multiple passes convergence) Deterministic code-path cascading (entries map themes codes) Code-aware sentiment analysis (sentiment scored coding, using codes context) Codebook-first learning prior studies (QDPX, Excel, CSV codebooks full theme/subtheme/code hierarchies) Researcher review points (pause coding theme generation) Checkpoint/resume long-running analyses Correlation analysis theme-sentiment relationships Publication-quality HTML report saturation curves, theme narratives, interactive tables

title: pakhom 1.0.0
text: Multi-provider AI support (OpenAI GPT-4o, Anthropic Claude Sonnet 4) Progressive sequential coding: entries processed one time growing codebook, just like human researcher NVivo. batch coding, separate deduplication consolidation steps Thematic saturation detection using triangulated signals: code creation rate monitoring (Guest et al., 2020), code reuse stability (De Paoli & Mathis, 2024 adaptation), AI self-assessment. Saturation curve saved publication Iterative bottom-theme generation: codes merged multiple sequential passes productive groupings exist. Themes subthemes emerge organically data Deterministic code-path cascading: entry--theme mapping flows code hierarchy (AI re-reading raw text), faithful inductive process Code-aware sentiment analysis: sentiment scored initial coding, using assigned codes context accurate emotional valence detection Codebook-first learning prior studies: full theme/subtheme/code hierarchy extraction QDPX files (NVivo exports), Excel, CSV codebooks. Manuscripts serve supplementary clarification codebook descriptions insufficient Cross-study qualitative synthesis: structural patterns identified across available prior analyses collectively (just sequence) Researcher review points: pause pipeline coding theme generation curate AI’s output continuing Checkpoint/resume system flat checkpoint architecture reliable resume across retries interruptions Inter-rater reliability: Cohen’s kappa Krippendorff’s alpha small-sample correction human verification AI-generated codes Reddit scraper OAuth authentication Rich HTML report interactive DataTables, saturation curves, sentiment-coded quotes, theme detail drill-downs, cross-run comparison dashboard Cross-run comparison module 7 analysis dimensions (sample overlap, sentiment drift, code stability, theme evolution, entry migration, correlation persistence, run dashboard) Dynamic token-aware batch sizing AI operations Interactive Shiny configuration wizard building config.yaml

title: pakhom 1.0.0
text: Fixed: thematic-saturation detection longer fires prematurely longer runs. previous implementation derived code’s birth-time--coded-entries per-checkpoint accumulator lists get reset every checkpoint interval; first reset signal collapsed toward zero saturation declared even novel codes still created. Now stored directly parallel code_n_coded_at_birth map moment code creation Added: analysis_schema_version field run_metadata.json. cross-run comparison module (compare_runs, compare_models, list_available_runs) now schema-aware: incompatible snapshots excluded clear warning rather silently NA-padded Removed: hardcoded medication-research framing sentiment cross-study-synthesis prompts. sentiment system prompt longer asserts “qualitative health research” / “clinical experiences / treatment effects” regardless user’s actual research domain; .synthesize_cross_study_patterns longer matches theme names six medication-specific regex categories injects unconditional medication-narrative-arc claim AI’s learning context. Replaced domain-neutral, evidence-based listing actual top-level themes prior codebook Removed: confidence substantive correlation analyses (prepare_correlation_data, compare_theme_groups). AI sentiment prompt elicits confidence emotion_intensity single call, co-vary design (r >= 0.83 across observed runs); reporting correlation finding misled readers. Confidence remains per-entry sentiment_scores.csv diagnostic Fixed: DT package (Suggests) now graceful fallback knitr::kable installed Fixed: .html_esc() now escapes single quotes prevent XSS HTML attribute contexts Fixed: Variable shadowing exported n_themes() function report builder Replaced library(tidyverse) targeted imports generated Rmd faster rendering Added error = TRUE generated Rmd chunks individual chunk failures don’t prevent report rendering Added warnings load_data() auto-excludes database tables Added small-sample-size warnings theme membership correlations Added methodological notes saturation criteria pipeline-wide multiple testing report appendix Standardized error messages validate_class() helper Added list_available_runs() convenience function cross-run comparison Added getting-started vignette (now also documents Reddit’s post-2025 Responsible Builder Program approval requirement optional scraper) Added config_wizard_app() interactive configuration building Removed leftover stale references pre-1.0 architecture’s removed pipeline steps (relevance filtering, batch coding, code consolidation, theme assignment) report’s learning- transparency section, methodology appendix, distributed default config template Cleaned R CMD check warnings: documented previously undocumented function arguments, declared previously implicit base-stats imports (complete.cases, shapiro.test, wilcox.test, fisher.test, chisq.test), removed non-ASCII characters R sources
